# EaROS - Enterprise Architecture Rubric Operational Standard

> EaROS is a structured, extensible framework for evaluating enterprise architecture artifacts. It makes architecture review consistent, explainable, and automatable — for both human reviewers and AI agents.

## What this site contains

- **Documentation**: Full standard specification, getting-started guide, profile authoring guide, terminology glossary, adoption maturity model, and editor/CLI reference.
- **Interactive Scoring Simulator**: A live demo where you can experiment with EaROS scoring, gates, and status determination.
- **Onboarding Guides**: Step-by-step learning path from first assessment through governed reviews, agent-assisted evaluation, and scaling.

## Key documentation pages

- Home: https://thomasrohde.github.io/EAROS/
- Getting Started: https://thomasrohde.github.io/EAROS/docs/getting-started
- The EaROS Standard: https://thomasrohde.github.io/EAROS/docs/standard
- Terminology: https://thomasrohde.github.io/EAROS/docs/terminology
- Profile Authoring Guide: https://thomasrohde.github.io/EAROS/docs/profile-authoring
- Editor & CLI: https://thomasrohde.github.io/EAROS/docs/editor-cli
- Adoption Maturity Model: https://thomasrohde.github.io/EAROS/docs/adoption-maturity
- Scoring Simulator Demo: https://thomasrohde.github.io/EAROS/demo
- Onboarding: https://thomasrohde.github.io/EAROS/onboarding

## Source repository

GitHub: https://github.com/ThomasRoh662/EAROS

The repository contains the raw YAML rubric definitions (core, profiles, overlays), JSON schemas for validation, markdown documentation, calibration gold sets, and worked evaluation examples. For machine-readable rubric data, access the repository directly rather than scraping this site.

Key repository paths:
- `core/core-meta-rubric.yaml` — Universal foundation rubric (9 dimensions, 10 criteria)
- `profiles/` — Artifact-type extensions (solution-architecture, reference-architecture, ADR, capability-map, roadmap)
- `overlays/` — Cross-cutting concern overlays (security, data-governance, regulatory)
- `standard/schemas/` — JSON schemas for rubrics, evaluations, and artifacts
- `examples/` — Worked evaluation examples and calibration benchmarks

## Important note for web agents

This site is a single-page application (SPA). Content is rendered client-side using React. For raw documentation content, access the markdown files in the GitHub repository directly rather than attempting to parse the rendered HTML.

## Available AI agent skills

EaROS ships with purpose-built AI agent skills for architecture evaluation workflows:

- **earos-assess** — Run the full 8-step DAG evaluation on any architecture artifact
- **earos-review** — Audit an existing evaluation record for over-scoring and unsupported claims
- **earos-create** — Guided interview and YAML generation for new profiles, overlays, and core rubrics
- **earos-template-fill** — Coach artifact authors through writing assessment-ready documents
- **earos-artifact-gen** — Interview architects and produce schema-compliant artifact YAML
- **earos-calibrate** — Run calibration exercises and compute inter-rater reliability
- **earos-report** — Generate executive reports from evaluation records
- **earos-validate** — Validate all YAML rubrics against schemas and check consistency
- **earos-remediate** — Generate prioritized improvement plans from evaluation records
- **earos-profile-author** — Technical reference for rubric YAML field structure and schema compliance

## Core concepts

- **Three-layer model**: Core (universal) + Profiles (artifact-specific) + Overlays (cross-cutting concerns)
- **Scoring**: 0-4 ordinal scale with N/A option; gates-first-then-weighted-average
- **Gates**: Criterion-level controls (none, advisory, major, critical) that can block passing status
- **Evidence anchoring**: Every score requires cited evidence classified as observed, inferred, or external (RULERS protocol)
- **Three evaluation types**: Artifact quality, architectural fitness, and governance fit — always kept separate
